event: Ukrainian-French co-production partnerships will create near-term demand spike for NDAA-compliant ESCs and motors as French defense firms seek U.S.-compatible supply chains — only 1 joint venture established vs 11 for Germany indicates acceleration phase beginning Q3 2026
evidence_basis: DefenseNews article from 2026-04-10 reports 24+ Ukrainian defense companies in Paris seeking co-production deals, with French firms lagging (1 JV vs Germany's 11). Ukrainian Council of Defence Industry CEO explicitly seeking European defense-industrial base integration.
probability_basis: Strong signal from primary source (CEO statement, specific JV counts), but timing inference based on typical procurement cycles for establishing new supply chains post-partnership formation
drivers: Ukrainian-French defense co-production partnerships seeking European integration
NDAA compliance requirements for transatlantic joint ventures targeting U.S. market

co_occurrence_signal.pattern: Joint ventures requiring dual-compliance supply chains create parallel demand in both European and U.S. markets

event: High-energy laser C-UAS deployment on US-Mexico border will drive Q3 2026 demand for specialized beam control components and targeting sensors — FAA landmark safety agreement removes regulatory barrier, DHS/DoD immediate deployment indicates 8-12 week component procurement window
evidence_basis: DefenseNews 2026-04-10: FAA-DoD landmark safety agreement reached, laser C-UAS validated for border deployment after New Mexico testing. DefenseScoop confirms system already shot down government drone Feb 25, indicating operational deployment imminent.
probability_basis: Near-certain deployment from FAA safety approval and existing operational testing, but component demand timing inferred from typical directed-energy system procurement cycles
drivers: FAA landmark safety agreement removes regulatory deployment barrier
Operational testing complete with validated safety controls for passenger aircraft
validates_flag_id: C-UAS laser system deployment on US-Mexico border accelerates Q2-Q3 2026

lead_time_signal.component: laser beam directors and targeting sensors

event: Navy underwater mine-clearance drone deployment in Strait of Hormuz creates immediate demand for UUV navigation and autonomy components — CENTCOM announced Saturday deployment indicates procurement already complete, validating Q2 2026 UUV component tightening
evidence_basis: DefenseScoop 2026-04-11: U.S. Central Command announced underwater drones joining mine clearance in Strait of Hormuz 'in the coming days' per Saturday social media post. Immediate deployment timeline indicates components already procured.
probability_basis: Verified deployment announcement from CENTCOM, but component-level impact inferred from announced deployment timing — does not specify component types or quantities
drivers: Iranian mine blockade of Strait of Hormuz creating 'most severe disruption to global energy supplies on record'
Navy immediate UUV deployment for critical mine clearance operations

co_occurrence_signal.pattern: Mine-clearance UUVs in contested waters require same INS/IMU components flagged in GPS-denied drone navigation demand

event: Validation: Hezbollah FPV escalation flag likely understates motor and ESC shortage severity — TWZ reports 'ramping up' attacks with existing prediction citing 'increasing' attacks, but pattern indicates sustained high-volume consumption beyond initial surge estimate
evidence_basis: TWZ 2026-04-10 reports Hezbollah 'ramping up FPV drone strikes on IDF armor and personnel' since 2024, describing 'uptick' and stating use 'has proliferated from Ukraine to battlefields across the globe.' Existing flag cites same source but may underestimate sustained demand vs one-time spike.
probability_basis: Primary source confirms escalation, but reassessment suggests existing 0.86 probability understates duration — 'ramping up since 2024' indicates multi-quarter pattern not captured in original short-term surge estimate
drivers: Hezbollah sustained FPV attack campaign against IDF ground forces in Lebanon
Global proliferation of FPV tactics creating parallel demand across multiple theaters
validates_flag_id: Hezbollah FPV drone attack escalation validates ESC and motor supply chain stress

co_occurrence_signal.pattern: One-way attack FPV creates non-recoverable component consumption — each strike permanently removes motor/ESC from supply chain unlike reusable platforms

event: F-16 Block 70 counter-UAS kills by Bahrain validate air-to-air missile demand against small drones — first confirmed Block 70 drone intercepts indicate AIM-9X or similar required, creating cost-effectiveness pressure for lower-tier interceptors like Coyote
evidence_basis: TWZ 2026-04-10 reports Bahrain Royal Air Force F-16D Block 70 scored 'first air-to-air kills' against two Iranian drones during April 2026 Middle East conflict. Article explicitly states this is 'first air-to-air kills by the latest Block 70 variant.'
probability_basis: Verified F-16 intercepts from primary source, but component-level procurement impact inferred from cost-effectiveness logic — article does not specify missile type or procurement plans
drivers: Iranian drone threat in Middle East requiring manned fighter intercepts
Cost-effectiveness pressure driving demand for dedicated counter-UAS interceptor systems
validates_flag_id: Coyote counter-drone launcher expansion to USS Carl M. Levin validates Raytheon production ramp

co_occurrence_signal.pattern: Using AIM-9X ($430K/unit) against sub-$10K drones creates unsustainable cost ratio, accelerating Coyote ($100K) and other tier-2 interceptor procurement
